messages.content: this text a part of an article , please summarize it: The study developed an empirical model using RBV to explore the role of physical IT infrastructure in in enabling DT in hospitals through IT capabilities dimensions and strategic alignment. Data collected from 124 hospitals showed that, first, physical IT infrastructure had only an indirect impact on digital transformation and strategic alignment, while its importance was emphasized in determining the dimensions of IT capabilities, and second, the dimensions of IT capabilities had a significant impact on digital transformation, both directly and indirectly through strategic alignment.

A study developed an empirical model using Resource-Based View (RBV) to determine the impact of physical IT infrastructure on digital transformation (DT) in hospitals. The model showed that physical IT infrastructure had an indirect impact on DT and strategic alignment, but its importance was emphasized in determining the dimensions of IT capabilities. Additionally, the IT capabilities dimensions had a direct and indirect impact on DT through strategic alignment. Data collected from 124 hospitals were used to develop the model.
please more summarize

The study created a model using Resource-Based View to analyze the impact of physical IT infrastructure on digital transformation in hospitals. The model found that the physical IT infrastructure had an indirect effect on digital transformation and strategic alignment by impacting the dimensions of IT capabilities. Furthermore, the IT capabilities dimensions had significant direct and indirect impacts on digital transformation through strategic alignment. The research was based on data collected from 124 hospitals.
edit : Data collected from 124 hospitals showed that physical IT infrastructure as an IT resource had an indirect impact on DT and strategic alignment by impacting the dimensions of IT capabilities.

Data collected from 124 hospitals showed that physical IT infrastructure had an indirect impact on digital transformation and strategic alignment by affecting the dimensions of IT capabilities, as it is considered an important IT resource.

revise : Data collected from 124 hospitals showed that physical IT infrastructure had an indirect impact on DT and strategic alignment by impacting the dimensions of IT capabilities, as it is considered an important IT resource.

Data collected from 124 hospitals demonstrated that physical IT infrastructure was deemed an essential IT resource that had an indirect impact on digital transformation (DT) and strategic alignment, as it influenced the dimensions of IT capabilities.

edit : Data collected from 124 hospitals showed that physical IT infrastructure had an indirect impact on DT and strategic alignment by impacting the dimensions of IT capabilities, as it is highlighted as an important IT resource.

Data collected from 124 hospitals revealed that physical IT infrastructure was emphasized as a significant IT resource that had an indirect influence on both digital transformation (DT) and strategic alignment by influencing the dimensions of IT capabilities.

combine : The study developed an empirical model using RBV to explore the role of physical IT infrastructure in enabling DT in hospitals through IT capabilities dimensions and strategic alignment. Data collected from 124 hospitals revealed that physical IT infrastructure was emphasized as a significant IT resource that had an indirect impact on DT and strategic alignment by impacting the IT capabilities dimensions.

The empirical model developed using RBV investigated the contribution of physical IT infrastructure to enabling digital transformation (DT) in hospitals through strategic alignment and IT capabilities dimensions. The data collected from 124 hospitals demonstrated that physical IT infrastructure played a vital role as an IT resource and had an indirect impact on both DT and strategic alignment through affecting the IT capabilities dimensions.

edit: The study developed an empirical model using RBV to explore the role of physical IT infrastructure in enabling DT in hospitals. Data collected from 124 hospitals revealed that this infrastructure was emphasized as a significant IT resource that affects DT and strategic alignment through the IT capabilities dimensions.

Using RBV, the study established an empirical model to analyze the contribution of physical IT infrastructure in facilitating digital transformation (DT) in hospitals. The findings revealed that physical IT infrastructure was regarded as a prominent IT resource that had an impact on DT and strategic alignment through the IT capabilities dimensions. The data used in the research was collected from 124 hospitals.
edit: Data of 124 hospitals revealed that this infrastructure was emphasized as a prominent IT resource that affects DT and strategic alignment through the IT capabilities dimensions.

Based on data collected from 124 hospitals, the study revealed that physical IT infrastructure was considered a significant IT resource and had a notable impact on digital transformation (DT) and strategic alignment by affecting the IT capabilities dimensions.
summarize it

The study used Resource-Based View (RBV) to develop an empirical model that examined the contribution of physical IT infrastructure to digital transformation (DT) in hospitals through strategic alignment and IT capabilities dimensions. Data collected from 124 hospitals highlighted that physical IT infrastructure is a critical IT resource that impacts both DT and strategic alignment via the IT capabilities dimensions.
edit: . However, over time, some of these capabilities may become commoditized and transform into mere IT resources
With time, certain IT capabilities may become standardized and lose their uniqueness, eventually becoming mere IT resources.

edit: The resource-based view (RBV) argues that IT capabilities are a source of competitive advantage that cannot be easily replicated by competitors, unlike IT resources. However, over time, certain IT capabilities may become standardized and lose their uniqueness, eventually becoming mere IT resources.

The Resource-Based View (RBV) theory asserts that IT capabilities provide a sustainable competitive advantage that cannot be effortlessly duplicated by competitors, in contrast to IT resources. Nevertheless, as time progresses, some IT capabilities may become standardized, losing their uniqueness and transforming into mere IT resources.
edit: However, over time, some IT capabilities may become standardized and lose their uniqueness, eventually becoming mere IT resources.
Over time, certain IT capabilities may become standardized, resulting in them losing their unique characteristics and eventually becoming ordinary IT resources.

edit: The resource-based view (RBV) argues that IT capabilities are a source of competitive advantage that cannot be easily replicated by competitors, unlike IT resources. Over time, some IT capabilities may become standardized and lose their uniqueness, eventually becoming mere IT resources.

RBV suggests that IT capabilities are a source of a sustainable competitive edge that competitors cannot easily replicate, while IT resources can be mimicked. Nonetheless, as time elapses, IT capabilities may become standardized, depriving them of their unique character and modifying them into everyday IT resources.
edit: Over time, some IT capabilities may become standardized and lose their uniqueness, eventually becoming mere IT resources.
With the passage of time, the standardization of certain IT capabilities may cause them to lose their distinctiveness and eventually transform them into ordinary IT resources.

edit : Physical IT infrastructure has traditionally been viewed as a capability in most studies, the exponential growth of digital technologies, including cloud computing, has led to questions as to whether this traditional perspective remains valid in the context of hospitals, particularly during the process of DT.

While physical IT infrastructure has long been regarded as a capability in various studies, there has been skepticism over its relevance to the hospital context, particularly in the context of DT. The rapid growth of digital technologies, such as cloud computing, has raised doubts about the traditional viewpoint on physical IT infrastructure.

As time progresses, certain IT capabilities may undergo standardization and lose their unique attributes, ultimately transforming into mere IT resources.

edit without rephrase '

The study employed Resource-Based View (RBV) to explore the role of physical IT infrastructure in enabling DT in hospitals through IT capabilities dimensions and strategic alignment. Data collected from 124 hospitals revealed that physical IT infrastructure was a crucial IT resource that impacted both DT and strategic alignment, albeit indirectly, through IT capabilities dimensions. However, as time progresses, certain IT capabilities may experience standardization and lose their unique characteristics, eventually transforming them into regular IT resources.
i mean the prior text

The Resource-Based View (RBV) theory states that IT capabilities provide a sustainable competitive advantage that competitors cannot effortlessly duplicate, whereas IT resources can be copied. However, some IT capabilities may undergo standardization over time, causing them to lose their distinctiveness and become conventional IT resources.
edit without rephrase: Over time, some IT capabilities may become standardized and diminish their distinctiveness, eventually becoming mere IT resources.
Over time, certain IT capabilities can become standardized, resulting in them losing their uniqueness and eventually transforming into average IT resources.

edit and improve: In today's competitive business environment, executives are increasingly confronted with new digital technologies that blur market boundaries and change the roles of players (Verhoef et al., 2021). These new technologies are not always aligned with current business needs, which has made digital transformation (DT) a key issue for executives  (Susanti et al., 2023).

The current business landscape presents executives with technological advancements that are redefining market boundaries and altering roles of stakeholders (Verhoef et al., 2021). However, the evolving technological landscape is not always in line with existing business needs, heightening the importance of digital transformation (DT) for executives (Susanti et al., 2023).
